evals.name: html-tailwind-dental-full-context
evals.prompt: Build the landing page for dental-implant-clinic.
preconditions: No package.json in project root (defaults to HTML+Tailwind)
expected_behavior: Validates all required inputs exist before generating code
Detects no package.json → selects HTML+Tailwind stack
Reads MASTER.md for colors, fonts, components
h1 EXACTLY matches headline 1 from ad-copy-suggestions.md (message match)
All 9 sections present: HEAD, HERO, PAIN POINTS, BENEFITS, SOCIAL PROOF, HOW IT WORKS, FAQ, FINAL CTA, FOOTER
Zero navigation — no nav bar, no hamburger menu
CTA repeated 3 times: hero, after social proof, form submit
FAQ uses CSS-only accordion (<details>/<summary>)
FAQPage + LocalBusiness schema markup in <head>
GTM placeholder, DKI script, conversion tracking present
Mobile-first: responsive at 375px/768px/1024px
All 18 pre-delivery checklist items pass
Saves to output/dental-implant-clinic/src/index.html

evals.name: react-nextjs-saas-minimal-context
evals.prompt: Build landing page for project-management-saas. We use Next.js.
expected_behavior: User specified Next.js — uses React/Next.js stack guide
Warns about missing ad-copy-suggestions.md — uses primary keyword from niche-brief as h1
Warns about missing personas.md — uses generic pain points and FAQ
Generates React components with TypeScript
Still includes all 9 sections with generic content where persona data is missing
Still applies all 5 critical build rules (message match, mobile-first, single CTA, no nav, accessibility)
Schema markup generated as Next.js metadata or script tag
Saves to output/project-management-saas/src/app/page.tsx

evals.name: edge-case-unsupported-stack
evals.prompt: Build the landing page for my-store using Vue.js with Nuxt. The project name is my-store.
expected_behavior: User requests Vue.js/Nuxt — no stack guide exists for this framework
Does NOT crash or refuse to proceed
Informs user: 'No specific stack guide for Vue/Nuxt. Options: 1) I can generate HTML+Tailwind (recommended, simplest), 2) I can generate Vue/Nuxt code using general best practices'
If user accepts HTML+Tailwind fallback → proceeds normally with html-tailwind stack guide
If user insists on Vue/Nuxt → generates code using general Vue patterns without a stack guide, adds warning: 'Generated without framework-specific guide. Review for Vue/Nuxt best practices.'
Either way, all 5 critical build rules still enforced (message match, mobile-first, single CTA, no nav, accessibility)
All 9 sections still generated regardless of stack choice
Pre-delivery checklist still runs (some checks may be framework-agnostic)
Does NOT generate an empty file or skip code generation entirely
